title: Obstructive Uropathy from BPH
chief_complaint: I haven't been able to pee for 24 hours. I feel like I have to go but nothing comes out. My bladder feels like it's going to burst. I have lower abdominal pain.

physical_exam.Abdomen: Palpably distended bladder extending to umbilicus (globular mass in suprapubic region). Dull to percussion. Tender.
physical_exam.Rectal: Markedly enlarged prostate (60g), smooth, firm, symmetric. No nodules. No tenderness.
physical_exam.Genitalia: Normal. No phimosis. No meatal stenosis.

hidden_data.renal_us: Bilateral hydronephrosis (severe). Bladder massively distended. Prostate enlarged (60g). No stones.
hidden_data.psa: PSA 4.5 (can be elevated in acute retention - recheck after catheterization)

correct_diagnosis: Acute Urinary Retention with Obstructive Uropathy and AKI from Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia
key_learning_points: Acute urinary retention = inability to voluntarily urinate despite a full bladder. Most common cause in men >50: BPH. Other causes: Urethral stricture, Prostatitis, Neurogenic bladder, Medications (anticholinergics, opioids, alpha-agonists), Constipation
Complications: Obstructive uropathy → post-renal AKI, hydronephrosis, UTIs, bladder stones. Chronic retention → detrusor muscle damage → permanent bladder dysfunction

Immediate management: URETHRAL CATHETERIZATION (16-18Fr Foley). If fails: Coude catheter (curved tip for enlarged prostate). If fails: Suprapubic catheter (percutaneous or open). Monitor for post-obstructive diuresis (massive urine output after relief - can cause dehydration and electrolyte imbalances). Gradually decompress (some debate - rapid vs gradual)

Post-obstructive diuresis: Physiologic (appropriate excretion of retained fluid/urea) vs Pathologic (impaired concentrating ability). Monitor urine output q1-2h, electrolytes q6h. Replace fluids (½ NS). If output >200mL/h for >2h: Match 50-75% of UOP with IVF

Medical management of BPH: Alpha-blockers (Tamsulosin 0.4mg daily - relaxes smooth muscle, improves symptoms within days). 5-alpha reductase inhibitors (Finasteride 5mg daily - shrinks prostate over 6-12 months, for prostates >40g). Combination therapy for large prostates

Trial of void (TOV) after 3-7 days of catheterization: Remove catheter, measure post-void residual. If voids successfully with PVR <150mL: Continue medical therapy. If fails TOV: Consider TURP (transurethral resection of prostate), Laser prostatectomy, or Urolift. Intermittent self-catheterization as bridge
